summary: Academy Award-winning actor Robert Duvall has died at the age of 95, his wife, Luciani Duvall, revealed Feb. 16 in a Facebook post.
story: Academy Award-winning actor Robert Duvall has died, his wife, Luciana Duvall, revealed Feb. 16 in a Facebook post. He was 95.
“Yesterday we said goodbye to my beloved husband, cherished friend, and one of the greatest actors of our time. Bob passed away peacefully at home, surrounded by love and comfort,” she began.

“To the world, he was an Academy Award-winning actor, a director, a storyteller. To me, he was simply everything. His passion for his craft was matched only by his deep love for characters, a great meal, and holding court. For each of his many roles, Bob gave everything to his characters and to the truth of the human spirit they represented.

In doing so, he leaves something lasting and unforgettable to us all. Thank you for the years of support you showed Bob and for giving us this time and privacy to celebrate the memories he leaves behind.”

Last month, Duvall shared a video his wife created in honor of his 95th birthday that featured clips of him over the years, beginning as a little boy and ending with him eating a cookie as an older man.
"Luciana surprised me with this wonderful birthday video in preparation for tomorrow’s big 95! Thank you all for the support all of these years," he wrote.

Duvall’s career began with an appearance in a 1960 episode of the TV series “The Robert Herridge Theater.” He appeared in multiple TV shows before he made his film debut as Boo Radley in the 1962 adaptation of the book “To Kill a Mockingbird,” which won three Oscars and would be the first of a long line of Oscar-nominated and winning movies featuring Duvall.

Duvall won his Academy Award for best actor in 1984 for “Tender Mercies.” He would be nominated seven other times, including his first for “The Godfather,” playing Tom Hagen, the adopted son of Marlon Brando's Don Vito Corleone. He would reprise the role in “The Godfather Part II.”

Duvall also earned Oscar nominations for “Apocalypse Now,” “The Great Santini,” “The Apostle” — which he directed — “A Civil Action” and “The Judge.” In all, he earned Oscar nominations in four different decades. He also starred in “M*A*S*H,” “The Conversation,” “Network,” “The Natural,” “Falling Down,” “Days of Thunder,” “Colors,” “Deep Impact” and “Gone in 60 Seconds.”
Duvall’s final film appearance was in 2022’s “The Pale Blue Eye.” His final onscreen credit was earlier this year in an episode of “The Culture Mafia.”

title: Kelly Clarkson Reflects on Past ‘The Voice’ Wins Ahead of Season 29: Exclusive
summary: Ahead of her grand return to “The Voice” for the show’s special “Battle of Champions” season, Kelly Clarkson looks back at her four past wins and unforgettable moments with Blake Shelton and Adam Levine.
story: Ahead of her grand return to “The Voice” for the show’s special “Battle of Champions” season, Kelly Clarkson is reflecting on her four past wins and unforgettable moments with Blake Shelton and Adam Levine.

For its 29th season, “The Voice” is switching up its format as only three coaches who have all previously won the show — Clarkson, Levine and John Legend — will be on the coaching panel for the first time in the competition’s history. In addition to seeing how the new changes impact the competition, fans are also looking forward to watching Clarkson, who hasn’t been on “The Voice” since Season 23, reclaim her spinning red chair.
In a TODAY.com exclusive clip previewing the season, Clark discusses returning for her 10th season and reminisces about her early days on the show.

“I’m so stoked to come back to ‘The Voice.’ Let’s take a look back at some of my favorite moments,” she says at the start of the video. Her trip down memory lane begins with Season 14, her first on the show. Clarkson pulled off an impressive victory when she won with then-15-year-old Brynn Cartelli, who was also the Grammy winner’s first-ever chair turn.

The following season, Clarkson won again, this time with another teenage phenom named Chevel Shepherd. Speaking about her back-to-back victories, Clarkson says, “I love working with young artists. I just think it’s fun because they’re still so creative and still, like, ready to try anything.”
In Season 21, Clarkson made history when her artist Girl Named Tom, made up of three siblings, became the first trio to win.

But, the “Since U Been Gone” singer reveals that her “most exciting” win was when Season 17 country music singer Jake Hoot made it all the way to the end. She says besting Shelton that season with a country artist made the win even better.

“He saw me when Blake was here. He saw me, and he picked me, and I love him. And then I took him to the winner’s circle. What? That’s right, Shelton!” Clarkson adds while playfully poking fun at the show’s “King of Country.”
She then watches a snippet from her first season on “The Voice” where Shelton mocked her for being inexperienced.

“I love seeing Blake say that I have zero experience on the show, and then I totally won,” she says and laughs. “There’s nothing better. The ultimate revenge of someone just thinking you can’t do something, and then you show them with a trophy.”
Reflecting more on her first season, Clarkson shares that she instantly felt welcomed into “The Voice” family.

She continues, “It was just a really good time. It was a fun experience. And, I give Shelton a lot of crap, but he did help make me feel at home right off the bat, with his brotherly-love jabs. Adam as well, because we had toured together. So I kind of knew people, too, coming in.”
Although Shelton departed “The Voice” after Season 23, Clarkson will soon reunite with Levine for “The Voice: Battle of Champions,” premiering Monday, Feb. 23 on NBC.

For Season 29, past contestants are returning for a second chance to claim “The Voice” trophy. The season will also feature many new twists, including the Triple Turn Competition, a Super Steal, an In-Season All-Star Competition and new voting rules.

title: When and Where to Watch the 'America's Next Top Model' Documentary
summary: A new docuseries about "America's Next Top Model" is sure to be on top of Netflix's most-watched titles when it hits the streaming service next week
story: A new docuseries about "America's Next Top Model" is sure to be on top of Netflix's most-watched titles when it hits the streaming service next week.
Netflix describes "Reality Check: Inside America's Next Top Model" as the "definitive, must-watch chronicle of 'America’s Next Top Model' — and the chaos in front and behind the camera."
Before its release, we're sharing everything you need to know to tune in to the highly anticipated docuseries.
What Was 'America's Next Top Model'?

"America's Next Top Model" was a reality series that aired between 2003 to 2018. Supermodel Tyra Banks was one of the creators of the series, which lasted 24 seasons. Banks served as host for all season except one.
The series featured aspiring models who competed for the title of "America's Next Top Model" and earned a plethora of prizes to jumpstart their modeling career.

"What started as a glamorous launchpad for aspiring models became a pop-culture juggernaut defined by explosive drama, public meltdowns, and controversies that still fuel viral moments today," a press release reads.
Who Appears in the Docuseries?
The docuseries includes interviews with former judges, contestants and producers.

Banks will appear alongside Jay Manuel, the show’s former creative director, Nigel Barker, the series’ former fashion photographer, J. Alexander (Miss J), the show’s former runway coach; and “America’s Next Top Model” co-creator Ken Mok.
The following former winners and contestants will also make appearances: Whitney Thompson, Giselle Samson, Shannon Stewart, Shandi Sullivan, Dani Evans and Keenyah Hill.
Netflix Has Released a Trailer for the Docuseries
The trailer begins with an interview with Banks, who says, "I haven't really said much, but now it's time."
Later on, Banks describes one her motivations for starting "America's Next Top Model," saying, "I wanted to fight against the fashion industry."
Several former contestants and winners recall their experience on the show then Mok appears and says, "That was a moment where I realized, 'Oh my God. I think we've built a monster.'"
As with many TV series, "America's Next Top Model" was mired in controversy at times, something the trailer alludes to while several people discuss the topics of body shaming and ethnicity.
Manuel appears and says, "I realized Tyra would do anything for the success of her show."
Banks then adds, "I knew I went too far."
Manuel suggests that some drama went down on set and says, "You wanna talk about being slapped across the face."
"We felt betrayed," Barker adds.
"It was very, very intense. But you guys were demanding it. And so we kept pushing," Banks says.

A press release for the series offers more insight into the docuseries and reads, "'Reality Check' exposes the show’s complicated legacy and asks a provocative question: how far are we willing to go for entertainment?"
When and How to Watch 'Reality Check: Inside America’s Next Top Model'
All three episodes of “Reality Check: Inside America’s Next Top Model” will hit Netflix on Monday, Feb. 16, 2026 at 12 a.m. PT / 3 a.m. ET.

title: ‘Jeopardy!’ Fans React to One of the ‘Best’ Episodes Ever After Contestant ‘Manifested’ Victory
summary: Jeopardy fans are in awe after Alison Betts pulled off a strategic move bringing her from third place to the winner by writing, “Who is I hope they both bet everything?”
story: Maybe Alison Betts should change her name to Alison Betts wisely.
Betts pulled off a stunning move on the Feb. 11 episode of “Jeopardy!” en route to winning the game after she was in third place going into Final Jeopardy.

Betts, a creative executive originally from San Jose, California, was competing against Isaac Hirsch and Josh Hill in the quarterfinals of the Jeopardy! Invitational Tournament. She had $3,200 going into the final round, while Hirsch and Hill each had $7,600.
The Final Jeopardy category was "1960s Novel Characters" and the clue was, “An article about autism in fictional characters included him, whose ‘reward’ is to have his brave act go unrecognized.”
The correct response was Boo Radley from “To Kill a Mockingbird,” but no one got it right.
Betts, who went first, provided a cheeky answer.
“Who is I hope they both bet everything?” she wrote, eliciting laughter from the audience.
“I don’t know if you mean you want them to bet everything and get it right or get it wrong,” host Ken Jennings said.
“The getting (it) wrong part is kind of key to my plan,” Betts said before revealing she had wagered $414, leaving her with $2,786.
Hirsch went next but guessed incorrectly and revealed he had wagered all $7,600, leaving him with zero.
“So far, Alison’s plan is working,” noted Jennings, who then moved on to Hill, who also guessed wrong.
Jennings revealed the correct answer and then revealed Hill had also bet all his money, leaving him with nothing and making Betts the winner.
“Alison just manifested a spot in the semifinals,” Jennings said, as she put her head on the podium and then in her hands in disbelief.
The clip appeared on the official “Jeopardy!” TikTok page, where fans were in awe of Betts.
“That’s actually so much aura for her,” one person wrote.
“Finally, someone who knows how to bet correctly when you’re in third,” someone else commented.
“Optimal Jeopardy strategy was for both tied players to bet it all. She was smart to recognize that,” another person wrote.
“This may genuinely be one of the best Jeopardy moments ever,” another user raved.
While Betts may have defied the odds, she did appreciate the effort from Hill and Hirsch.
“Josh and Isaac were AMAZING and bet perfectly, for what it’s worth!!” she wrote on Instagram.

title: James Van Der Beek Gave Eric Andre a Life-Changing Pep Talk. What He Told Him About Self Doubt
summary: Eric Andre shares the advice James Van Der Beek gave him, helping him beat self-doubrt and believe he truly belonged in show business.
story: Eric André is remembering a moment of affirmation that helped silence the imposter syndrome and self-doubt that shadowed the earliest days of his career.

Following the death of his former co-star James Van Der Beek, who died at age 48 after a battle with stage 3 colorectal cancer, the comedian and actor recalled a private exchange that, he said, became a lifeline as he entered entertainment industry.
In an Instagram video posted Feb. 12, André said he was financially struggling and newly arrived to one of his first professional sets, “Don’t Trust the B---- in Apartment 23.”
“I was so nervous every day,” André said.
Van Der Beek offered words that would fundamentally reshape how he saw himself.

“Hollywood is not casting actors out of sympathy, you got here for a reason,” Van Der Beek told him. “It’s because you’re talented and you’re smart and you’re funny and you belong here. You belong on the soundstage. You belong on this lot.”
“And I’ve taken that with me through my entire career,” André said, describing the reassurance as something he returned to whenever insecurity threatened to take hold.
He described Van Der Beek as “the sweetest, most selfless, altruistic guy ever,” noting that the father of six continued to check in “year after year… just out of the kindness of his own heart.”
Van Der Beek also joined André on "The Eric André Show" in 2013.
On Feb. 11, Kimberly Van Der Beek announced her husband’s passing in a poignant post on Instagram, writing that, “he met his final days with courage, faith and grace.”
Van Der Beek was best known for his role as Dawson Leery on the iconic teen drama “Dawson’s Creek,” which ran from 1998 to 2003 and became a defining series for a generation of viewers.
In September, the cast reunited in New York City for a one-night only event, though Van Der Beek was forced to cancel at the last minute due to “two stomach viruses.”

He appeared instead via a pre-recorded message, taking a moment to thank everyone involved in the show and to acknowledge the devoted viewers who had supported it over the years, calling them “the best fans in the world.”

title: ‘The Traitors’ Season 4, Episode 9 Recap: Who Was Banished and Who Was Murdered?
summary: Was new Traitor added to the turret? Read on for a recap of Episode 9 to find out. Plus, learn if the Faithfuls banished a Traitor for the third straight week.
story: Warning: Spoilers from Season 4, Episode 9 of “The Traitors.”
After the Faithfuls successfully sniffed out the identity of two Traitors two weeks in a row, OG Traitor Rob Rausch was the only one remaining in the turret…but maybe not for long.

When Episode 9 of the Peacock series began, the “Love Island USA” star, who turned on fellow Traitors and “Real Housewives” alums Lisa Rinna and Candiace Dillard Bassett, had carried out the task he received from host Alan Cumming at the end of the previous episode: recruit one of the Faithfuls to join him as a Traitor or die. Rausch chose singer Eric Nam to deliver the ultimatum to and shocked the musician when he revealed his true, treacherous identity.

So does Rausch have a new ally in the turret to bring to the end of the game? Or did Nam decide to die a Faithful? Read on for a recap of Episode 9 to find out. Plus, learn if the Faithfuls banished a Traitor for the third straight week.
A New Traitor In the Turret?

Episode 9 picks up with Nam’s reaction to Rausch being a Traitor. Rausch offers Nam a chance to team up with him and says they can win together. The suspense is short-lived as Nam instantly wants to grab his cloak and head to the turret. “Alright, I’m in,” he tells Rausch.

But, Nam isn’t sure he will be able to pull off deceiving the others. “I need a helmet and an adult diaper because I’m about to s--- my pants and my head’s about to explode. This is so stressful,” he says in his confessional. He also points out that although he is looking forward to working with Rausch, he could end up being betrayed like Rinna and Dillard Bassett.

In the turret, Rausch notes that he and Nam only have to survive three more Round Tables to win the game. The duo discuss their shortlist for murder: “The Real Housewives of New York City” alum Dorinda Medley, “Survivor” winner Natalie Anderson and “One Tree Hill” star Stephen Colletti.
The Faithfuls Shift to a New Target

“Top Chef” host Kristen Kish, “Dancing With the Stars” pro Mark Ballas and Rausch are the first trio to arrive for breakfast the next morning. Kish and Ballas discuss their theory that Olympic figure skater Johnny Weir is the remaining Traitor. They also wonder if he recruited fellow Olympic figure skater and his broadcasting co-host Tara Lipinski to the turret. When Weir enters the breakfast room, he senses that he is the next player on the chopping block.
The players discover that Medley is the latest casualty of the Traitors.

“I mean, I’m very sad but you know what they say: Curiosity kills the cat,” Medley says in a confessional after learning her fate. “I’m sure I’ve put a lot of faith into some of you, Traitors. And you know what, I would have loved to have stayed on, but, you know, the queen’s got to get back to Bluestone Manor.”

After breakfast, the players start throwing out more names. Anderson thinks Colletti’s reactions at breakfast were fake and Kish directly tells Lipinski she believes she is a Traitor. The others are still convinced Weir cannot be trusted. But Lipinski has his back, saying in a confessional, “They picked the wrong girl to mess with their best friend.”
A Risky Mission

For the mission, all the players have to climb into a box and close the lid. Inside, they will select the name of a player they think the Traitors chose as the answer to a question asked by Cumming. But, the Traitors weren’t given the questions ahead of time. So, when all the players enter their boxes, the Traitors have to sneak out, agree on their answer and then stealthily return to their boxes. The players have an opportunity to add $18,000 to the prize pot during this mission.

After hearing the first question — which player is the most useful for the Traitors —Rausch and Nam pick Lipinski for their answer. They then continue to choose her for every answer so that the Faithfuls don’t gain any helpful information. It takes the Faithfuls a few rounds before they figure out the plan. They only win $5,500.

The last question will determine one Faithful’s fate in the game. “Who do the Traitors want to shortlist for murder tonight?” Cumming asks. The four players chosen will be up for murder. Rausch wants to throw off the Faithfuls and suggests to Nam that the two of them don’t put themselves on the shortlist, assuming the Faithfuls will wrongly guess that a Traitor is in one of the boxes. So, Rausch and Nam decide to put Anderson, Weir, Lipinski and Kish at risk.
An (Undercover?) Friendship Is Revealed at the Round Table

This far into the game, the players are unaware how close Lipinski and Weir are as the two have been downplaying their friendship. Realizing that Weir could be banished, they decide to reveal their relationship, hoping it will make them seem more trustworthy to the others.

Lipinski begins the Round Table discussion by speaking for herself and Weir, promising that they are both Faithfuls. The two say they blindly trust each other before suggesting Colletti is a Traitor. Weir also mentions Dillard Bassett pulling Colletti for a private conversation last episode as part of his evidence.
Colletti says their reasoning is “weak,” but Anderson is also suspicious of him. Meanwhile, Kish and Ballas are convinced Weir and Lipinski cannot be trusted.
Before the vote begins, Cumming asks if the player with the dagger wants to use their special power. Anderson tells Rausch to announce to everyone he has the dagger, and he does.
Most of the players vote for Colletti, banishing him from the castle. Weir and Lipinski receive two votes each.

While on the Circle of Truth, the actor says, “I just want to say it’s been the opportunity of a lifetime to play this game with all of you. I’ve been very excited to be here, eager to play the game and, maybe, sometimes a little bit that eagerness probably got the best of me. I respect you all, but I am a Faithful and you blew it.”

Many of the players are bummed, but Kish is irritated. She confronts Nam for not contributing during the Round Table. Then, she pulls Ballas and Rausch to the side and tells them she doesn’t trust Nam.

The episode ends with Nam and Rausch in the turret going through the pros and cons of murdering the four players who are vulnerable. Cumming informs viewers that the four players will be woken up in the middle of the night and sent to the castle dungeon. Then in Episode 10, out Feb. 19, they will each turn the handle of a jack-in-the-box. If a traitor pops out of the box, then that player will be murdered.

title: 'Love Story': What to Know About Carolyn Bessette's Sisters, Lauren and Lisa
summary: Who are Carolyn Bessette's sisters, Lauren and Lisa Bessette? Lauren Bessette was in the plane that killed Carolyn Bessette and JFK Jr.
story: Tragedy struck in 1999 when Carolyn Bessette and John F. Kennedy Jr. died in a plane accident.
Kennedy was piloting the single-engine plane when it crashed into the Atlantic Ocean on July 17, 1999, killing him, his wife and his sister-in-law, Lauren Bessette, who was also aboard.

When Carolyn Bessette started dating Kennedy, she was thrust into the public eye. Not as much was known about her two sisters, twins Lauren and Lisa Bessette, though they were also impacted by the relationship.

A new FX series, "Love Story," chronicles Kennedy and Carolyn Bessette's relationship and their very public life. Before the series premieres on Feb. 12 on Hulu on Disney+, we're sharing everything you need to know about Carolyn Bessette's sisters.
Carolyn Bessette Had Older Twin Sisters
Carolyn Bessette's older sisters, Lauren and Lisa Bessette, were fraternal twins. They were born on Nov. 5, 1964.
In her book, “Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy,” which inspired “Love Story,” Elizabeth Beller says the twins "both excelled academically" in school.
The twins were also close with their younger sister, who was born on Jan. 7, 1966. Lauren and Lisa Bessette took their role as older sisters seriously and "looked out for Carolyn," per the book.
The 3 Sisters Had Different Academic Pursuits
In her book, Beller describes Lauren Bessette as "very studious and goal-oriented." Meanwhile, Lisa Bessette "seemed more sensitive and bohemian, interested in art."
Lauren Bessette graduated from Hobart and William Smith Colleges with a degree in economics and was a member of the Omicron Delta Epsilon honor society.
Lisa Bessette attended the University of Michigan and earned a degree in art then went on to pursue a Ph.D. in art history.
Carolyn Bessette, meanwhile, got a degree in early childhood education from Boston University but went into publicity and fashion instead. She worked for Calvin Klein when she met Kennedy.
Both Lauren and Lisa Attended Carolyn's Wedding
When Carolyn Bessette and Kennedy said "I do," they only invited a small group of friends and loved ones in an effort to maintain their privacy from the paparazzi.
In Beller's book, readers learn that Bessette twin sisters attended the intimate celebration along with their mother, stepfather and one of their stepsisters.
Lauren and Carolyn Grew Closer While They Both Lived in New York
Lauren Bessette spent four years working at Morgan Stanley's Hong Kong Branch, per Beller's book, but returned to New York, where Carolyn Bessette lived with Kennedy.
During this time, the sisters became "even closer."
Lauren Worked for Morgan Stanley

Following college, Lauren Bessette got a job at Morgan Stanley and was later promoted from vice president to a principal, per the New York Times. While working for the company in New York, she was a member of the investment banking division.
Lauren Bessette also spent some time working in Hong Kong and put her expertise in Asian financial markets to use. As part of her work, she worked as a venture capitalist and set up investment deals.
Lauren Died in the Plane Crash with Her Sister and Brother-in-Law

On July 16, 1999, Bessette-Kennedy, Kennedy and Lauren Bessette were en route to Martha's Vineyard in Kennedy's plane. The couple was going to drop Lauren Bessette off there then proceed on their way to his cousin's wedding weekend.
When the plane crashed, all three passengers died. Five days later, their bodies were recovered from the shattered fuselage.
Lisa Is the Sole Remaining Sister
Lisa Bessette is not in the public eye. In Beller's book, the author describes Lisa Bessette as "the quietest of the three."
The book also reveals that Lisa Bessette studied in Munich while working on her dissertation for her Ph.D. in medieval art history.

"Lisa was shyer and quieter than both Carolyn and Lauren, but she shared their intellectual curiosity and parlayed the same drive with which her sisters rose to the tops of their fields into her work as an art historian," the book reads.

title: Allison Holker Announces Engagement to Adam Edmunds
summary: Allison Holker is engaged to her boyfriend, tech CEO Adam Edmunds
story: Allison Holker is engaged to her boyfriend, tech CEO Adam Edmunds.
The dancer shared the news on her Instagram Feb. 12, sharing a series of photos from the proposal. In the snaps, she's wearing a satin red dress, while her new fiancé is in a black suit.

"We’re ENGAGED! It was the most romantic night of my life! I am so in love with you Adam," she began in the caption. "Baby, I am forever grateful for you and the impact you have had in my life and my kids lives."

"I am a better person because of you. You helped me find me again and showed me how to love. Every morning I wake up I feel safe knowing you are my person at my side," she continued. "You and your kids coming into our lives has been the biggest blessing."
She went on to share how he surprised her with a birthday party and had invited all her loved ones to be by her side as he popped the question.
"The whole night was magic and you had every detail thought out to make it even more special. Thank you for bringing all the people we love together to celebrate our love," she wrote, in part.
She also shared that he brought out musician Clinton Kane to sing "I Guess I'm In Love With You" at the party, and then added lyrics from the tune to the caption.
She concluded by writing, "I love you Adam. And thank you all for your love and support!"

Holker was previously married to the late Stephen “tWitch” Boss, who died on Dec. 13, 2022, and they shared three children together. Boss adopted her daughter Weslie, and they welcomed son Maddox and daughter Zaia together.
Holker first teased her new relationship on her Instagram page in August 2024. At the time, she only shared a photo of her shadow holding hands with a mystery man.
The two would later step out for their first public appearance at  the Alice + Olivia by Stacey Bendet spring 2025 collection show in New York City.

title: Taylor Swift Congratulates Olympian Breezy Johnson on Her Engagement With Sweet Song Lyric
summary: Breezy Johnson is celebrating her engagement with family, friends and fans — including Taylor Swift.
story: Breezy Johnson is celebrating her engagement with family, friends and fans — including Taylor Swift.
After the skier’s super-G run during the Milan Cortina Olympics on Feb. 12, her boyfriend, Connor Watkins, got down on one knee and proposed.

The newly-engaged twosome and Johnson’s fellow USA teammates celebrated the emotional moment together at the finish line. Shortly after, Johnson shared the news on her Instagram, introducing her fiancé.

“Hey guys... meet Connor! My ex boyfriend! And current fiancé!!!” she wrote, alongside photos of the proposal and her ring. “Thank you to everyone who made this moment possible. It was everything I ever dreamed of. Kind of like Connor!”

One of the pictures in the post showed Johnson holding a ring box with an inscription that read “Honestly, who are we to fight the alchemy?” Those are lyrics from Taylor Swift's love song “The Alchemy.”
So, Swift decided to pop into the comments section and congratulate the happy couple.
“‘Where’s the trophy? He just comes running over to me’ CONGRATULATIONS!!!” the superstar wrote, quoting more lines from the song from her “The Tortured Poets Department” album.
Johnson reposted Swift’s comment on her Instagram story, and wrote, “I am deaddddddd.”
“The Alchemy” is about a love that “happens once every few lifetimes,” with fans believing it to be about the pop icon’s romance with fiancé Travis Kelce.

The full lyrics are: “Where’s the trophy? / He just comes running over to me / Touch down/ Call the amateurs and/ Cut ’em from the team/ Ditch the clowns, get the crown/ Baby I’m the one to beat/ Cause the sign on your heart/ Said it’s still reserved for me/ Honestly, who are we to fight alchemy?”
Johnson has been having one memorable 2026 Winter Olympics. On Feb. 8, she won Team USA’s first gold medal of the Winter Games after dominating the alpine skiing women’s downhill.
And while she crashed during the Feb. 12 super-G final, she ended the day with another shiny trophy — her blue stone engagement ring.

Speaking to TODAY after her fall and engagement, Johnson said, “When you make mistakes like that in ski racing you kind of feel stupid and you just kind of want to go talk to your friends and family. And, yeah, seeing him was really exciting. And then just everything else is crazy.”
Watkins, who was by Johnson’s side during the interview, added that he felt a bit anxious before he proposed.

“It’s an incredibly surreal experience,” Watkins said. “I’d been really nervous. Every time she goes down the hill, I’m nervous, right? … She got up after the crash. It was good, and that was a sigh of relief. And then my heart started racing again, because I knew I was about to go and get down on my own knee and propose.”

title: What ‘Love Island’s’ Kordell Beckham Thinks of Co-Star Rob Rausch on ‘The Traitors’
summary: Kordell Beckham weighs in on Love Island co-star Rob Rausch's time on The Traitors Season 4.
story: Warning: This article contains spoilers for Season 4 of "The Traitors" so far.
Rob Rausch is making a killing on "The Traitors" — literally.

Rausch was among three contestants selected by host Alan Cumming to play the reality competition show as a Traitor, meaning he's responsible for duping the "Faithful" surrounding him as he helps pick them off one by one while surviving banishments at the roundtable.

Rausch has played as a Traitor since the Season 4 premiere alongside "Real Housewives of Potomac" star Candiace Dillard Bassett and "Real Housewives of Beverly Hills" star Lisa Rinna — both of whom has been banished after he helped turn the tables on them.

But Rausch has continually evaded suspicion, with "Top Chef" host Kristen Kish, a Faithful, going as far as saying in the most recent episode, "there's no way that sweet, sweet man" could be a Traitor.
And Rausch's "Love Island USA" Season 6 castmate Kordell Beckham couldn't be prouder.
TODAY.com caught up with reality star Beckham, the brother of NFL player Odell Beckham Jr., in  San Francisco ahead of Super Bowl 60 at the annual Leigh Steinberg Super Bowl party.

Beckham confirmed he is watching "The Traitors" (though noted TikTok spoiled the stunning events of Episode 8, when Rausch was left as the last Traitor standing and had to recruit another player to join him).
"I'm proud, that's all I know," he said with a grin.
Asked if Rausch's approach to the game has surprised him, he answered immediately: "No, not at all," he said with a laugh.

Beckham and Rausch were both original islanders on Season 6 of the Peacock dating show. While Rausch was eliminated on Day 30 of "Love Island USA," Beckham went all the way coupled up with Serena Page. The two won each other's and viewers' hearts over the course of the 32-day season — and they're still going strong.
"Everything's going good, I mean we're still going strong," Beckham tells TODAY.com. "Our anniversary is coming up soon ... and Valentine's Day is coming up soon."
As for what he has planned?
"Shhhh," she responds. "I don't want her to find out."
(Peacock is owned by NBCUniversal, TODAY's parent company.)

title: 'Heated Rivalry' Author Rachel Reid Weighs in on More Viral Theories, Including a Standalone 7th Episode and If Scott Knows
summary: Rachel Reid weighs in on viral theories about Heated Rivalry like if Scott knows about Shane and Ilya and a 7th Heated Rivalry episode.
story: Rachel Reid knows you have "Heated Rivalry" theories — and on the latest episode of "Open Book with Jenna," she weighed in on a few of them.

Reid is the author of the "Game Changers" romance novel series. Already beloved among romance readers, the story catapulted to a new level of pop culture fame with the TV adaptation of "Heated Rivalry."
Produced by Crave, a Canadian production company, the series aired stateside on HBO Max in late November and, over its six-week run, became a massive hit.
Since then, Reid's books have hit the New York Times bestseller list, "Heated Rivalry's" stars have gone to the Golden Globes and the Olympics — and fans clamor for more.
Will "Heated Rivalry" continue? Yes, it was renewed for a second season midway through airing (which Reid hints will return in spring 2027) and a third book en route about Shane and Ilya.
But how about the rumored seventh episode?
READ: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Author Rachel Reid Addresses If ‘Unrivaled’ Is the Last Book About Shane and Ilya

In the wake of Season 1's ending, some "Heated Rivalry" fans held out hope that a standalone "seventh" episode was coming before the second season. But some fans were left bereft when another Thursday arrived and there was no new episode of “Heated Rivalry.”
The rumors go back to comments Bell Media VP Justin Stockman gave to the Toronto Star about creator Jacob Tierney moving forward on a potential standalone episode

"We’re going to see what’s feasible. If (Jacob) can find a way to make the source material work in a way that gets us something sooner or something special, I’m sure he will. I would love to have this back for the holidays next year, but that likely is not going to happen. We can’t rush the process. We won’t be able to repeat the magic if we push him," he said.
When asked by Jenna about the possibility, Reid said: "It's a surprise for me if there is (an episode)," she said.

Reid also weighed in on another viral debate among fans: Does fellow hockey player Scott Hunter (Francois Arnaud) know about rivals Shane Hollander (Hudson Williams) and Ilya Rozanov's (Connor Storrie) relationship?
There are countless TikToks analyzing moments that Scott seems to detect something going on between Ilya and Shane.

First, Scott notices that Ilya gives Shane his hotel number. Scott's hotel number was next door, meaning he probably knew what "1221" meant. Later, before a fight on the ice, Scott tells Shane, “You’re starting to sound like him,” referring to Ilya.
Layered into this is Scott's own romance with a man, Kip, which takes center stage in Episode 3 and a triumphant Episode 5.
On this debate, Reid does have an opinion.
"I think on the show (Scott) does. Not in the books," she said. "But the show version of him is clocking in. Seems like it."
Scott's story unfolds in "Game Changer," the first book in Reid's series, and in the first season of "Heated Rivalry."

Listen to the full conversation between Jenna and Reid here, which delves into the pop culture phenomenon of the "cottage," the unexpected place she gets her writing done and what's next for the series.

title: Olympians Had to Pronounce Their Own Names for the Olympics Website and People Can't Get Enough
summary: These are the best instances of Olympians pronouncing their own names from the 2026 Winter Olympics.
story: With athletes from all around the world competing at the 2026 Winter Olympics, pronouncing their names can get a little tricky at times. Luckily, the official Olympics website has a handy way to help you make sure you're not butchering anyone's moniker.

Before the games began, athletes recorded the proper pronunciation of their names. Now, visitors to the official Olympics website can visit each athlete's biography and click on their name to hear it out loud.
The Olympics even shared a video rounding up some of the athletes teaching fans how to say their names.
Over the last few days, the recordings have gone viral on social media, with many people sharing their own video mashups featuring different athletes pronouncing their names.
For instance, TikTok user @melissaontherecord had a bit of fun and added commentary and emojis to some of the athletes' recordings.
When cross-country skier Andrew Musgrave sped through his name, the TikTok user added a runner emoji to the video.

The TikTok user added a frustrated gif and the word "How?" when Australian snowboarder Cameron Bolton shared an unconventional pronunciation of his name. Several commenters also got a kick out of it, with one commenting, "So glad Cameroni Baltoni translated his name into Italian." Another wrote, "Cameroni has me in genuine tears."
TikTok user @lovingwallows also shared a compilation of the athletes' recordings and added the following caption to the first few: "pronouncing their names totally normal."
In response to Macklin Celebrini's pronounciation, the TikTok user wrote, "add some character to it."
Canadian ice hockey player Emma Maltais' pronunciation is also getting a round of applause from the internet, with comments zeroing in on how differently she says it each time.
Other recordings also catch technical difficulties, like American hockey player Grace Zumwinkle letting out a big laugh before saying her name.
This isn't a new feature for the Olympics. The 2026 Games have inspired social media users to find their favorite athlete name pronunciations from past games.
There have been plenty of other standout moments from the Winter Olympics in the last week.

Team Canada figure skater Madeline Schizas made headlines for emailing her professor to ask for an extension on an assignment. While preparing to compete, the McMaster University senior mixed up the deadline and explained the situation to her professor. To her delight, he granted the extension.

Norway’s Sturla Holm Lægreid also made jaws drop when he made a shocking confession after taking home a bronze medal in the 20 km biathlon. The athlete revealed that he had cheated on his girlfriend three weeks ago and told her a week ago.

“My only path to the finish line is to tell her everything and put it all on the table, and hope that she’ll still love me. I’ve done that for her, and now for the entire world. I’ve nothing to lose,” he said.

title: Katie Holmes Writes a Handwritten Tribute to Co-Star James Van Der Beek After His Death at 48
summary: The cast of “Dawson’s Creek” are celebrating James Van Der Beek’s legacy following the news of the actor’s death at the age of 48.
story: The cast of “Dawson’s Creek” are celebrating James Van Der Beek’s legacy following the news of the actor’s death at the age of 48.
On Wednesday, Feb. 11, the “Varsity Blues” star’s wife, Kimberly Van Der Beek, wrote in an Instagram post that her husband had died.

“Our beloved James David Van Der Beek passed peacefully this morning,” she said in a joint post that included James Van Der Beek’s Instagram account. “He met his final days with courage, faith, and grace. There is much to share regarding his wishes, love for humanity and the sacredness of time. Those days will come. For now we ask for peaceful privacy as we grieve our loving husband, father, son, brother, and friend.”

James Van Der Beek publicly revealed that he had been diagnosed with colorectal cancer on Nov. 3, 2024. “I’ve been privately dealing with this diagnosis and have been taking steps to resolve it, with the support of my incredible family,” he said in a message.

After the post announcing his death was uploaded, many of James Van Der Beek’s co-stars throughout the years paid tribute to him, including the actors who shared the screen with him on “Dawson’s Creek.”
Katie Holmes posted a handwritten note to Instagram Feb. 12, writing in her caption, "I formed some words with a heavy heart."
Holmes played Van Der Beek's character's best friend and longtime love interest Joey Potter, who appeared in all six seasons of the teen drama.
She opened her note by simply writing, "Thank you," to Van Der Beek.

"I mourn this loss with a heart holding the reality of his absence and deep gratitude for his imprint on it," she added. "To Kimberly and the children, we are here for you always. And will always be there to shower you with love and compassion."
Busy Philipps, who played Audrey Liddell, shared throwback photos from their time on set together.

“My heart is deeply hurting for all of us today…every person who knew James and loved him, anyone who loved his work or had the pleasure of meeting him, all of his dear friends and community that surrounded him as he battled this illness, especially his parents and brother and sister,” she wrote. “But I am profoundly heartbroken for his incredible wife Kimberly and their six magical children.”
She mentioned the family’s GoFundMe page and said she would link to it in her Instagram story.

“James Van Der Beek was one in a billion and he will be forever missed and i don’t know what else to say,” she added. “I am just so so sad. He was my friend and i loved him and i’m so grateful for our friendship all these years.”

Chad Michael Murray, who briefly portrayed Charlie Todd for 12 episodes, commented beneath the Instagram post about James Van Der Beek’s death and said, “Sending love and light to your beautiful family. James was a giant. We’re so so so sorry for what you’re going through. His words, art and humanity inspired all of us — he inspired us to be better in all ways. God bless you guys.”

“Dawson’s Creek aired for six seasons on The WB from 1998 to 2003. In September, the cast reunited for a one-night only event in New York City. James Van Der Beek canceled the day before due to “two stomach viruses.”
He sent a surprise pre-recorded message instead to express his gratitude to the everyone who contributed to the show, including the fans.
“From the cast to the crew to everybody who’s doing anything and has been so generous, and especially every single last one of you,” he shared. “You are the best fans in the world.”

title: 'Love Story': How Did John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette Really Meet?
summary: How did John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette meet? There are a few versions of the love story.
story: Even though it's been nearly 30 years since John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette died in a tragic accident, their love story still endures — and fascinates.
A new FX series series titled "Love Story," out Feb. 12, revisits the couple's relationship from courtship to marriage and gives viewers a fictionalized look into their highly public lives.

Episode 1 dives into the start of their relationship. According to the show, they meet at a black-tie event. But is that really the case? Here's what's been said about how the real JFK Jr. and Bessette met and started dating.
There Are Multiple Versions of JFK Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's Meet-Cute
In a documentary titled “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes,” subjects recall varying versions of how Bessette and Kennedy might have met.
“There’s one account of how both were running in Central Park and he was suddenly taken aback by her beauty,” The Knot Senior News Editor Esther Lee said in one interview.
Meanwhile, Kennedy’s close friend Billy Noonan seemed to think that one of Kennedy's friends introduced the lovebirds.
J. Randy Taraborrelli, author of "The Kennedy Heirs," acknowledged that the stories vary depending on who you ask.
"I tried to stay away from it in my book because everybody I talked to had a different story," he said.
Lee mentioned another account about "a friend of hers and his going suit shopping."
"Yeah, that sounds possible. The jogging in the park thing, no," Kennedy’s friend Sasha Chermayeff said in the documentary.
Either way, Taraborrelli said Kennedy "swept her off her feet very early on in their relationship."

"Carolyn was used to guys who were more closed and she had to draw out. John was not that kind of person. If John was interested in you, he was very open and very forthcoming, and she found that to be very refreshing," he said.
The Couple Met for the First Time in 1992
In her book, “Once Upon a Time: The Captivating Life of Carolyn Bessette-Kennedy,” which inspired “Love Story,” Elizabeth Beller details another version of the story of how the couple met.
The author says Kennedy booked a fitting in Calvin Klein's VIP room in spring 1992, and Bessette was selected to handle the important meeting.

"So even though Carolyn usually only showed the women's line, Calvin ... decided it should be she, the most effervescent person on the salesfloor, who would show John the selection of clothing. Unsurprisingly, John came out of the meeting smitten, with a few men's suits and Carolyn's phone number. He called within days," the book reads.

Beller writes that Kennedy invited Bessette to attend a gala dinner and sit at his table with the rest of his group. However, it didn't go well since another woman was sitting next to Kennedy and Bessette assumed she was his date.
"It was unclear, and Carolyn wasn't pleased. When John invited her to the after-party at a local club, she coldly said, 'I can't. I'm meeting people,' and abruptly left," the book reads.
After reflecting on the night, Bessette later wrote a thank-you note to Kennedy to smooth things over. Unfortunately, he didn't call right away.
While attending a fundraiser in May 1992, Kennedy and Bessette crossed paths again.

Kennedy's friend Sasha Chermayeff recalled the encounter in the book, saying, "Eventually I spotted Carolyn. Just by looking at her I realized he has brought me here to see her, to tacitly approve... There was something captivating about the way she moved, the tilt of her head. Carolyn was captivating. Without John even looking her way, my eyes went to her, and I knew."
Per Beller’s book, Kennedy and Bessette dated for a few months before breaking up temporarily.
The Couple Dated in 1994 and Got Engaged in 1995
The couple reconnected in 1994 and started dating again. However, they had a major obstacle to confront: Kennedy wouldn't introduce Bessette to his mother.
Per Beller's book, Bessette broke up with Kennedy "a couple of times" for not introducing her to his mother, Jacqueline Kennedy Onassis.
Eventually, their relationship settled down and Kennedy proposed in 1995. Three weeks later, Bessette accepted his proposal, per “JFK And Carolyn’s Wedding: The Lost Tapes.”
The Couple Got Married in 1996
Bessette and Kennedy tied the knot in an intimate ceremony on Cumberland Island, a small island off the coast of Georgia, in September 1996.
They died in a plane crash three years later.

title: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Author Rachel Reid Addresses If ‘Unrivaled’ Is the Last Book About Shane and Ilya
summary: “Heated Rivalry” author Rachel Reid addresses if she has plans for more books about Shane and Ilya after "Unrivaled," and if she know show their story ends.
story: “Heated Rivalry” fans are waiting more chapters in Shane Hollander and Ilya Rozanov’s relationship — and the new pages might not be the last.
Rachel Reid, author of “Heated Rivalry” and the “Game Changers” hockey romance series, joined Jenna Bush Hager on a bonus podcast episode of “Open Book with Jenna,” out Feb. 12.

Steamy and heartfelt, Reid's book was adapted for the screen into a six-episode series. “Heated Rivalry” sparked a phenomenon and launched actors Connor Storrie and Hudson Williams to fame when it premiered on HBO Max in November.

Then in January, Reid announced Shane and Ilya’s love story would continue on the page in her upcoming book, “Unrivaled,” which marks the third book about the couple and the seventh book in the “Game Changers” series.
When asked if “Unrivaled” would be the final book about Shane and Ilya, Reid left the door open.
“I don’t want to say last,” she told Jenna. “You know, I thought ‘The Long Game’ was the last book, so you never know.”
That said, Reid is keeping a satisfying ending in mind as she finishes up “Unrivaled.”
“I try to write these books as if they could be the last book,” Reid said. “So I do try to wrap things up — but you know, you never know.”

“Unrivaled,” out Sept. 29, follows "Heated Rivalry's" sequel, “The Long Game." When Shane and Ilya’s relationship is accidentally exposed, the former rivals get to be in a public relationship for the first time. According to the synopsis, Shane and Ilya are enjoying married life and playing on the same hockey team, but backlash to their relationship in the sports world is brewing.
As she writes "Unrivaled," Jenna asked if Reid has an ending in mind for Shane and Ilya.
“I have in my head an idea of where they are, say, 10 years later,” Reid said.
However, Reid said she is not disclosing if she will complete that timeline in “Unrivaled.”

Meanwhile, the show is operating on a different timeline. Season 1 followed the events of Reid's first book about Shane and Ilya, and the already-renewed Season 2 is expected to incorporate the plot of “The Long Game.”
While Reid couldn’t outright confirm a Season 2 premiere date, she said of a spring 2027 return, “That is what I believe to be true.”
Jenna, on her end, is excited for more.
“Can’t wait for ‘Unrivaled,'" Jenna said. "We can’t wait for Season 2, and we’re just so happy for you — that people are falling in love with this world you’ve created and these characters."
Listen to the full "Open Book with Jenna" episode featuring Rachel Reid here.

title: U.S. and Iran tout progress in nuclear talks as supreme leader warns Trump over military buildup
summary: The United States and Iran have reached an understanding on the “guiding principles” in nuclear talks, Tehran’s foreign minister said Tuesday, though he cautioned that work still needs to be done to reach an agreement and head off the threat of an American military attack.“I believe we made good progress,” said Abbas Araghchi, the head of the Iranian delegation in Geneva.
story: The United States and Iran have reached an understanding on the “guiding principles” in nuclear talks, Tehran’s foreign minister said Tuesday, though he cautioned that work still needs to be done to reach an agreement and head off the threat of an American military attack.

“I believe we made good progress,” said Abbas Araghchi, the head of the Iranian delegation in Geneva. “The path toward an agreement has started but we will not reach it quickly,” he told state media after hours of indirect talks, adding that the two sides would separately work on draft texts before a new round of negotiations.
A U.S. official echoed his comments, telling NBC News that “progress was made, but there are still a lot of details to discuss.”
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google
Vice President JD Vance gave a mixed review of the talks today in an interview with Fox News.

"In some ways, it went well. They agreed to meet afterwards," Vance said. "But in other ways, it was very clear that the president has set some red lines that the Iranians are not yet willing to actually acknowledge and work through. So we’re going to keep on working it."

Trump’s special envoy, Steve Witkoff, and son-in-law Jared Kushner have been leading the U.S. delegation at the talks, which are being mediated by Oman. The Iranian delegation “said they would come back in the next two weeks with detailed proposals to address some of the open gaps in our positions,” the U.S. official said.

The positive comments from Tehran came as a massive American military buildup was accelerating, as President Donald Trump seeks to pressure the Islamic Republic into a new nuclear deal in the wake of its deadly crackdown on nationwide unrest.

Trump has said “regime change” in Tehran may be the best thing that can happen, but Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei said Tuesday that any U.S. attempts to depose his government would fail.

Just as the talks got underway in Geneva, Iranian state media reported that parts of the strategic Strait of Hormuz would close for a few hours due to “security precautions” while Iran’s elite Revolutionary Guard conducts military drills there.
Tehran has threatened to shut down the strait to commercial shipping if it is attacked in the past, a move that would choke off a fifth of global oil flows and drive up crude prices.

“We have entered certain details related to both the lifting of sanctions and nuclear subjects,” Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Esmaeil Baghaei told state media before they ended. “We are ready to continue these talks as long as it takes. The issues are complicated. There is no trust between the two parties. We have to continue the negotiations in these conditions,” he added.
Baghaei has said Tehran’s views on the nuclear issue, the lifting of economic sanctions and a framework for any understanding have been conveyed to the U.S. side.
Trump said Monday that he would be involved “indirectly” in the Geneva talks and that he believed Tehran wanted to make a deal.

“I don’t think they want the consequences of not making a deal,” Trump told reporters aboard Air Force One. “We could have had a deal instead of sending the B-2s in to knock out their nuclear potential. And we had to send the B-2s.”
The U.S. joined Israel last June in bombing Iranian nuclear facilities.

Since the June strikes, Iran’s rulers have been weakened by street protests, suppressed at a cost of thousands of lives, against a cost-of-living crisis driven in part by international sanctions that have strangled the country’s oil income.

Just after the talks started, Iranian media cited 86-year-old Khamenei as saying Washington could not force out his government. The republic has been ruled by clerics since the 1979 Islamic Revolution.
“The U.S. President says their army is the world’s strongest, but the strongest army in the world can sometimes be slapped so hard it cannot get up,” he said, in comments published by Iranian media.

Washington has sought to expand the scope of talks to nonnuclear issues, such as Iran’s missile stockpile. Tehran says it is willing only to discuss curbs on its nuclear program — in exchange for sanctions relief — and that it will not give up uranium enrichment completely or discuss its missile program.
Khamenei reiterated Iran’s position that its formidable missile stockpile is nonnegotiable and that their type and range have nothing to do with the United States.

Tehran and Washington were scheduled to hold a sixth round of talks in June last year when Washington’s ally Israel launched a bombing campaign against Iran and was then joined by U.S. B-2 bombers that struck nuclear targets. Tehran has since said it has halted uranium enrichment activity.
Tuesday’s meeting took place at the residence of the Omani ambassador to the United Nations amid a heavy security presence. Some cars with Iranian diplomatic license plates were visible outside.

The U.S. and Israel believe Iran aspires to build a nuclear weapon that could threaten Israel’s existence. Iran says its nuclear program is purely peaceful, even though it has enriched uranium far beyond the purity needed for power generation, and close to what is required for a bomb.

Iran has joined the nuclear Non-Proliferation Treaty, which guarantees countries the right to pursue civilian nuclear power in return for requiring them to forgo atomic weapons and cooperate with the U.N. nuclear watchdog, the International Atomic Energy Agency.
Israel, which has not signed the NPT, neither confirms nor denies having nuclear weapons, under a decades-old ambiguity policy designed to deter surrounding enemies. Scholars believe it does.

title: Robots and Draco Malfoy: How China is celebrating the Year of the Horse
summary: BEIJING — The Lunar New Year is coming, and China is on the move
story: BEIJING — The Lunar New Year is coming, and China is on the move.

Hundreds of millions of people are traveling to their hometowns or making overseas trips ahead of the Feb. 17 holiday, known in China as Spring Festival, in what is considered the world’s largest annual human migration.

They will make a record 9.5 billion trips during the 40-day period around the holiday, up from 9.02 billion last year, according to the National Development and Reform Commission. That includes 540 million trips by train and 95 million by air, with the vast majority traveling by road.

For many workers living far from home, it’s the one chance they get each year to take a break and spend time with family. This year’s festival, which ushers in the Year of the Horse in the Chinese zodiac, starts Sunday with nine days of public holiday, one more than usual.
Xu Zhanyu, 41, a finance worker in Beijing whose hometown of Tangshan is about 100 miles away, said that although he can visit “any time,” Spring Festival is special.
“The biggest thing is the family reunion,” he said in an interview  Wednesday outside the bustling Beijing Railway Station.
“It’s the only time you can let go of everything and just be with the people you love and those who love you. You don’t have to think about anything else.”

title: Why a dart frog poison believed to have killed Alexei Navalny points to the Kremlin
summary: Precise, deniable and, in Russia’s case, grimly familiar.
story: Precise, deniable and, in Russia’s case, grimly familiar. Poison has become less a medieval cliché than a geopolitical signature flourish in recent years.

Accusations of Russian poisoning surfaced again this week after Western governments said laboratory analysis found the rare frog-derived toxin epibatidine, a compound associated with Ecuadorian poison dart frogs, in samples from the body of Russian opposition leader Alexei Navalny.

The allegation feeds into a long and deeply contested narrative around high-profile poison cases in Vladimir Putin’s Russia, from radioactive tea to nerve agents. Moscow has consistently denied involvement in the episodes, which have shaped its global reputation.

A joint statement by the U.K., France, Germany, Sweden and the Netherlands on Saturday said forensic tests concluded that epibatidine, a powerful neurotoxin, was present in Navalny’s system after his death in a Siberian penal colony in 2024.

Russia’s prison service reported in February 2024 that Navalny, 47, died after having felt unwell following a walk around the high-security facility in a remote town above the Arctic Circle where he was serving a combined 30½-year jail sentence.

British officials said only the Russian government had the capability and opportunity to deploy the toxin against Navalny, and they have reported the case to the Organization for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons. French Foreign Minister Jean-Noël Barrot said that the episode shows Vladimir Putin is willing to use chemical agents against his own citizens to maintain power.
U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio said at a news conference Sunday he is aware of the “troubling” findings and has no reason to question them.
Russian Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said Saturday that Russia would comment “where there are test results, where there are formulas of the substances.”

While Russia dismisses the allegations, the case has focused attention on the extraordinary lethality of the substance involved, an exotic toxin whose potency and synthetic accessibility make it a weapon that experts say can be produced and deployed only by a state with advanced chemical capabilities.

Epipedobates anthonyi, known as Anthony’s poison arrow frog, typically measures 22 mm long. Its skin carries sufficient epibatidine to kill a human several times over, with lethal doses measured in minuscule amounts as little as 1.4 micrograms.
The drug “is not naturally found in Russia,” the British foreign ministry said in a joint statement Saturday, but its absence in nature is irrelevant when a state is able to reproduce and deploy it.

“The structure is known and it’s possible to synthesize it chemically, so you wouldn’t have to go to Ecuador looking for brightly colored frogs, wash them down and get the toxin off their skin,” Alastair Hay, a professor of environmental toxicology at the University of Leeds, told NBC News.
“You could make it in the lab,” he said.

title: An artist, a geophysicist and a fruit seller: Accounts of Iran's brutal crackdown emerge
summary: With Iran still reeling from a brutal crackdown on protests, details about victims are trickling out and the world is gradually getting a clearer picture of the violence used.
story: A fruit seller and father of two killed during his first protest. A biotechnology graduate with a passion for art who bled to death in her father’s arms. A distraught family ordered to pay morgue officials $7,000 for a loved one’s body unless they lie and say their relative died at the hands of anti-government rioters.

These are a tiny fraction of the thousands of Iranians killed or wounded when the government cracked down on protests a month ago. With the nation still reeling, details about victims are trickling out and the world is gradually getting a clearer picture of the violence used to suppress the nationwide demonstrations.

Most of the killing happened during a two-day period between the night of Jan. 8 and Jan. 10, with over 7,000 people killed across the country, according to the U.S.-based Human Rights Activists News Agency.

“This was a very rapid 48-hour massacre. I can’t think of anything in Iran’s own history that’s comparable, unless I go back to the 18th century,” said Hadi Ghaemi, executive director of the Center for Human Rights in Iran, a New York-based advocacy group.

The demonstrations, sparked in late December as the rial currency crashed and inflation soared, turned into one of the biggest challenges faced by the Islamic Republic in its 47-year history as thousands of people across the country, including members of the country’s many ethnic minority groups, took to the streets to demand an end to clerical rule.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

Communicating with journalists can be very dangerous for protesters’ families, and Iran is in the middle of a communications blackout with severe restrictions on the internet and cellphone service. So to report on those killed by security forces, NBC News relied on sources outside Iran who were in touch with the families of victims inside the country.
These are the stories of four killed during January’s carnage.
Negin Ghadimi

Negin Ghadimi studied biotechnology, but her real passion was art. In a video posted on Instagram, 26-year old Ghadimi shows a sketch of a woman’s dress covered with mirrors that she has designed. She wanted people to see their own reflections, she said.
“My view of my future is very bright,” Ghadimi, a former competitive swimmer, says in a separate Instagram video.

title: Republican congressman's anti-Muslim remark prompts calls for his resignation
summary: Rep. Randy Fine, R-Fla., prompted calls for his resignation from Democrats and a major Islamic civil rights group after suggesting in a social media post that he'd choose dogs over Muslims."If they force us to choose, the choice between dogs and Muslims is not a difficult one," Fine posted to the social media platform X on Sunday.
story: Rep. Randy Fine, R-Fla., prompted calls for his resignation from Democrats and a major Islamic civil rights group after suggesting in a social media post that he'd choose dogs over Muslims.
"If they force us to choose, the choice between dogs and Muslims is not a difficult one," Fine posted to the social media platform X on Sunday.

Shortly afterward, he added a photo of a post from Nerdeen Kiswani, the co-founder of the pro-Palestinian group Within Our Lifetime, in which she called dogs "unclean" and said that "NYC is coming to Islam." Kiswani later told NBC News in an email she made the comment satirically.
Fine wrote in the follow-up post, "For context, this is the leader of one of the key mainstream Muslim groups that supported Mamdani," referring to Zohran Mamdani, New York City's new mayor.
Fine's initial post sparked a parade of criticism and a handful of calls for him to resign.

In a statement, the Council on American-Islamic Relations pointed to its previous condemnation's of Fine's past comments on Gaza and Hamas, adding that "leaders of Congress across the political spectrum should demand his resignation, which is long overdue.”
House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries, D-N.Y., in a statement called Fine "an Islamophobic, disgusting and unrepentant bigot."

"It is unacceptable that Mike Johnson and House Republicans continue to remain silent. Their casual acceptance of hateful and divisive language enables this out-of-control behavior. Republican leaders must hold this so-called Member of Congress accountable," he said. "Political violence, xenophobia and hate crimes against the Muslim community are on the rise. House Democrats will not let the racist and bigoted behavior of Randy Fine go unchecked. Accountability is coming to all of these sick extremists when the gavels change hands in November, if not sooner."
Rep. Robert Garcia, D-Calif., called the post "disgusting Islamophobia and dangerous bigotry," adding that "he should be forced to resign" after his committee assignments are stripped from him.
Rep. Yassamin Ansari, D-Ariz., called for House Speaker Mike Johnson, R-La., to "reprimand him immediately," adding that he's "repeatedly dehumanized Muslims without consequence."
California Democratic Gov. Gavin Newsom responded to Fine on X, writing, “Resign now, you racist slob.”
Kiswani replied to her own message from two days earlier by evoking Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem’s comments in her 2024 book about killing a dog on her farm.

“Kristi Noem literally bragged about shooting her own dog and most of you did not bat an eyelash. A Muslim in NYC says maybe the city is not the best place for pets and it is days of death threats from MAGA and Zionists,” she wrote.

Kiswani told NBC News in an email that her original comment was “satire” based on a “hyper local NYC conversation” about dog waste in the city after the recent snowstorm, adding she was “satirizing Islamophobic hysteria portraying Mamdani’s mayoralty as a societal takeover.”

She went on to accuse Fine of having a “documented pattern of dehumanizing Palestinians and Muslims” and said that “there has been a troubling pattern in which anti Muslim and anti Palestinian rhetoric from elected officials has not been met with the level of accountability we would expect if almost any other community were discussed this way.”
Fine’s and Johnson's offices did not immediately respond to NBC News' requests for comment.

Fine hasn’t balked amid the criticism. He continued to double down on his original comments Tuesday in an interview on conservative media outlet Newsmax. "My post was in response to a major Muslim leader saying dogs should be forbidden from New York City because to some Muslims, it bothers them. Well, if they're going to make us choose between our dogs and them going home, the choice is easy," he said, sharing the clip on his X account.

Fine also repeatedly posted images on X of dogs on a bright yellow background over the words "Don't Tread On Me," styled to resemble the Gadsden flag, iconography that dates to the Revolutionary War. He replied to a critical message from broadcaster Piers Morgan by writing that "what is disgusting is a major NYC Muslim leader saying we must give up our dogs because 'NYC is coming to Islam.'"
"We will not be shamed into being conquered like the Europeans," Fine added.

The Republican congressman, who took office less than a year ago in a special election, is an ardent supporter of Israel who has repeatedly defended the country's conduct during its war with Hamas in Gaza. He also faced criticism, including from then-Rep. Marjorie Taylor Greene, R-Ga., for posting on X last year that until Hamas released Israeli hostages, Gazans should "starve away."

title: Future kings meet in Saudi Arabia but Epstein revelations loom over Prince William
summary: Prince Williams trip to Saudi Arabia to meet with Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman came at a crucial time for the U.K. but the former Prince Andrew loomed.
story: LONDON — One wore a meticulously tailored navy blue suit. The other, a long robe traditional to the men of his desert kingdom. Both are set to rule their respective nations.

As Britain’s Prince William was led by Saudi Arabian Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman on a tour around the mud brick At-Turaif District — a UNESCO World Heritage site and the birthplace of the first Saudi state — they seemed at ease in each other’s company.

But the three-day visit, requested by the British government aiming to strengthen ties with a key Middle Eastern ally and major regional player, was seen by some as a tricky diplomatic mission for the future king as he attempts to build his personal profile at a torrid time for his family as it faces near daily revelations about the former Prince Andrew’s relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.

“This is part of his elevation to heir to the throne,” said Craig Prescott, who teaches law at Royal Holloway, University of London, and specializes in the constitutional and political role of the monarchy. This made it clear, he added, that William was second only to his father, King Charles III.

Ahead of this week’s trip, William will undoubtedly have been briefed about Saudi Arabia, which has been widely criticized for its human rights record, its suppression of women and where protest and political dissent are punished.

And he will also have been given guidance on his fellow prince, who unlike William is not part of a constitutional monarchy answerable to lawmakers and open to criticism from an often boisterous free press.
Known by his initials, MBS, Crown Prince Mohammed is Saudi Arabia’s de facto leader and a controversial figure with a checkered and bloody history, and the son of King Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud.
After the gruesome 2018 killing of Saudi journalist Jamal Khashoggi in his country’s consulate in Istanbul, the crown prince became an international pariah.

U.S. intelligence agencies concluded the crown prince approved the killing — a claim he has denied — although in 2019 he said that he took “full responsibility” for it since it happened on his watch. A Saudi court sentenced five people to death for their involvement in the killing.

Bin Salman was also widely criticized for the war in Yemen where fighting between his Saudi-led coalition and Houthi rebels led to one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises. While a truce was declared in April 2022, a comprehensive peace deal is yet to be struck.

“I don’t think it’s incumbent on members of the British royal family to have to deal with issues like human rights,” Ed Owens, an author and royal historian, said in a telephone interview earlier this week.

Instead, he said, William’s visit was “a clever ploy on the part of the British government to dispatch someone who can bring meaning to the relationship, but doesn’t have to engage with difficult issues around what happened to Jamal Khashoggi or other human rights abuses.”
Kensington Palace said in a statement that William would meet young Saudis and hear “about the country’s ambitious plans for the future, including its commitment to sustainability.”

But Owens said that he thought the aim of the British government, which secured nearly $9 billion in mutual trade and investment deals last year and “has significant interest in the Middle East and in Saudi, is for the future king to forge a lasting relationship with another future king,”

Together with wife, Catherine, the Princess of Wales, William played a crucial role when President Donald Trump made a historic second state visit to the United Kingdom in September; Owens said William’s visit is the continuation of that soft power at play.
“There is an emphasis here on William wielding not just his national popularity, but a global renown and popularity in the singular interest of Britain,” he added.

As well as the visit to At-Turaif, William was taken to a soccer training session for girls, a relatively new phenomenon in a country where women have only been given more freedom in recent years, including the right to drive in 2018, although huge limitations remain. He also visited a sustainable farm.

But throughout his visit, remained the specter of his uncle, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, who has been stripped of his titles and royal mansion because of his relationship with Epstein. British police said Monday that they were assessing whether the former prince leaked sensitive trade documents to the disgraced financier and convicted sex offender.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

Mountbatten-Windsor, who has always denied any wrongdoing in connection with his affiliation with Epstein, reached a legal settlement with Virginia Roberts Giuffre, one of the most prominent survivors of Epstein’s sexual abuse, in 2022. She repeatedly alleged that the financier trafficked her to his powerful friends, including Andrew, before her death in April.
In their first public statement on the scandal Tuesday, a spokesperson said William and Kate were “deeply concerned” by the continuing revelations and “their thoughts remain focused on the victims.”
“In terms of the timing, I think it was to try and get those questions out of the way so the focus can be on the Saudi trip,” Prescott said.
But the scandal followed William to Saudi Arabia anyway.
Twice from the sidelines of a soccer pitch in Riyadh, a reporter asked to what extent the royal family had done enough about the issue. William did not respond, but it was unclear whether he heard.
It is highly unlikely Crown Prince Mohammed would have faced similar questions.

title: Warmer words but relations remain frosty between the U.S. and its old friends in Europe
summary: From Munich to Bratislava and Budapest
story: From Munich to Bratislava and Budapest.

The United States’ top diplomat left a major European security gathering over the weekend to a standing ovation, but he soon gave a clear signal of what America now sees as friendly territory on the continent.

Secretary of State Marco Rubio proclaimed a “golden age” Monday in U.S. relations — not with Germany or France, but with Hungary, as he met with the country’s strongman Viktor Orbán, widely considered Russian President Vladimir Putin’s closest partner among all European Union leaders.
A day earlier, he met with Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico, another populist opposed to support for Ukraine and aligned with the MAGA agenda.

That central European swing through Trump-friendly capitals followed Rubio's Valentine's Day appeal to old bonds at the Munich Security Conference, where he struck a warmer tone after months of mounting antagonism between America and its longtime allies.
Add NBC News to Google
Add NBC News to Google

“We belong together,” Rubio told the audience of European leaders and policymakers who gathered in Munich on Saturday still reeling from the clash over Greenland and the sense they could no longer rely on Washington’s support for their security.
“For us Americans, our home may be in the Western Hemisphere, but we will always be a child of Europe,” Rubio added to applause.

On the surface, this message stood in stark contrast to the verbal attack on European values made by Vice President JD Vance at the same gathering last year. But many experts and observers argued that the message delivered by Rubio was not a shift in policy, but a sugar-coated version of the U.S. approach that has fueled talk of a "rupture" with its longtime allies.

“The Rubio speech marked a deliberate contrast to Vance’s broadside at European cultural decline,” Bronwen Maddox, director and chief executive of the London-based think tank Chatham House, wrote in an analysis. “But there was a clear warning that the Trump administration would go its own way in pursuit of U.S. interests if it did not find Europe sympathetic.”
European leaders seem to be under no illusion.
“A gap, a deep divide, has opened up between Europe and the United States of America,” German Chancellor Friedrich Merz told the conference Friday.

Danish Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen, meanwhile, expressed doubts in Munich that Trump had abandoned his aggressive pursuit of Greenland, the NATO member's semi-autonomous Arctic territory. “Unfortunately, I think the desire is the same,” she said.

“Our allies no longer trust us,” Sen. Mark Kelly, D-Ariz., wrote in a post on X after Rubio's speech. “I know there was celebration at the end of the Munich Security Conference. Unfortunately the champagne corks were popping in Beijing and Moscow,” he added.

title: British airline bans two 'disruptive' passengers for life after midair brawl
summary: British airline Jet2 announced that it banned two travelers for life after they started brawling on a flight bound for Manchester, England, on Thursday, resulting in its diversion
story: British airline Jet2 announced that it banned two travelers for life after they started brawling on a flight bound for Manchester, England, on Thursday, resulting in its diversion.
The flight was traveling from Antalya, Turkey, when the midair fight broke out, according to the airline.
The cause of the brawl was not immediately clear Saturday.
The flight was diverted to Brussels, Belgium’s capital city, as a result of the disruption, the airline said.
Jet2, a budget-friendly airline behind last year’s viral “Nothing Beats a Jet2 Holiday” jingle, called the behavior of the two passengers “appalling.”

“They were both offloaded by police in Brussels and the flight continued to Manchester,” the airline said in the statement. “We can confirm that the two disruptive passengers will be banned from flying with us for life, and we will also vigorously pursue them to recover the costs that we incurred as a result of this diversion.”
Videos depicting the altercation could be seen circulating online. In one video, two men start exchanging blows before additional passengers get involved. Many could be heard urging the men to stop.

“As a family friendly airline, we take a zero-tolerance approach to disruptive passenger behaviour, and we are very sorry that other customers and our colleagues onboard had to experience this too,” the airline said.

title: Ancient Egyptian artifacts stolen from Australian museum
summary: He turned “cat burglar” into a job description
story: He turned “cat burglar” into a job description.
A 52‑year‑old man has been charged after an Egyptian cat sculpture and “priceless” artifacts were stolen from an Australian history museum, Queensland police said Sunday.
The suspect is accused of smashing a window early Friday at the Abbey Museum of Art and Archaeology in Caboolture, north of Brisbane, before making off with the ancient haul.
